author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
dharmaniṣṭhā nāhi mora, ātmabodha vā sundara,
bhakti nāhi tomāra caraṇe
atoeva akiñcana, gatihīna duṣṭajana,
rata sadā āpana vañcane

patita⋅pāvana tumi, patita adhama āmi,
tumi mora ekamātra gati
tava pādamūle painu, tomāra śaraṇa lainu,
āmi dāsa, tumi mora pati

e bhaktivinoda kā̐de, hṛde dhairya nāhi bā̐dhe,
bhūme paḍi’ bale ataḥpara
ahaitukī kṛpā kari’, ei duṣṭajane hari,
deha pada⋅chāyā nirantara
en_translation:
O Hari! I have no religious steadfastness, nor self-realization, nor devotion at Your lotus feet. Therefore, I am a wicked person without possessions or refuge, always engaged in self-deception.

You are the purifier of the fallen, and I am a fallen, lowly soul. You are my only shelter. I have fallen at Your lotus feet and taken shelter. I am Your servant, and You are my master.

Bhaktivinoda cries, no patience remains in his heart; falling to the ground, he says: granting causeless mercy, O Hari, to this wicked person, always give the shade of Your lotus feet.
